The '''New Threats''' was a campaign against the [[Nami]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Background==
Rogue [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]] had betrayed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the [[Zaarin insurrection]], Zaarin attempted to strip the Empire of its starfighter superiority by stealing the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] technology from his [[Research]] platform. The attempt was unsuccessful and two remaining TIE Defender prototypes were safely uploaded to a [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] [[Mharsup]] and the staff, including the [[Unidentified TIE Defender chief scientist|chief scientist]] onto the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|Lambda-class shuttle]] ''[[G'nabgib]]''. After transferring the fighters to the corvette group [[Hashim group|Hashim]]. However, the corvettes were ambushed and disabled by [[Rneekii]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. Although driven off, the pirates made off with the scientist and the data. Worse was that another pirate force, the [[Nami]], led by [[Ali Tarrak]], managed to steal one of the prototypes, meaning that it was possible for TIE Defenders to be manufactured outside the Empire's authority. This situation was unacceptable to the Empire, especially with the upcoming confrontation with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]].

==The conflict==
===Ransom===
The Rneekii decided to [[Ransom/Legends|ransom]] the scientist to the highest bidder. Unsurprisingly, the Empire was the highest bidder and agreed to trade the scientist at [[Kiilimaar]]. Unfortunately for the pirates, they underestimated the Empire's resolve to recover the scientist as the single [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboat]] they allowed as escort was piloted by [[Tan (title)|Tan]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]], who after receiving the signal "Full speed to Kiilimar!" attacked the pirates' transport and disabled it, with the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor]] ''[[Red Claw]]'' trapping the pirates and preventing their escape. While the pirates were being dealt with, a small Nami strike force with [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 transport]] ''[[Dhoora]]'' arrived and attacked the ''Red Claw'', disabling the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] and the [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well generator]]. The strike team boarded the shuttle to try to capture the scientist, but the operation was unsuccessful, and the transport was captured and taken to the ''Red Claw'' so the crew could be interrogated.

===Rescue===
With the hyperdrive and gravity well disabled, the ''Red Claw'' called two [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|modified corvettes]] which formed flight group [[Chemeti group|''Chemeti'']] to pick up the prisoners and the scientist and deliver a new hyperdrive engine. The [[Chemeti's impostor|first ''Chemeti'']] arrived saying the second had been delayed. Zaarin suddenly sent a strike force of multiple TIE Defenders, which was thwarted by Maarek Stele as the corvette retrieved the scientist and prisoners and left. Not long afterwards, two corvettes from ''Chemeti'' arrived, and at that point the Imperials realized that they had been deceived as Zaarin's [[Strike-class medium cruiser]] ''[[Z-Kopsh]]'' arrived to destroy the ''Red Claw''. The attack was thwarted by Stele, who guarded the ''Red Claw'' at the captain's preference to abandon the ship.

===Under the Gun===
Maarek Stele was then sent to the [[Yrrna system/Legends|Yrrna system]], where an Imperial cargo area was under attack from TIE Defenders built by Ali Tarrak. Tarrak also arranged for [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|Carrack-class light cruiser]] [[Jester (Carrack-class)|''Jester'']] to observe the attack. While the cargo area was being destroyed, the [[Modular conveyor]] ''[[Ulzepdi]]'', which carried the surviving personnel, managed to get a safe distance from the TIE Defenders but was being pursued by [[R60 T-wing interceptor|T-Wings]] and [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|transports]]. Stele provided cover for the transport by destroying all T-wings and transports before they could damage the ''Ulzepdi''.

===Missile Boat Diplomacy===
[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] spoke to [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and introduced to him his personally designed starfighter, the [[Missile Boat]]. Maarek Stele was among the Imperials to pilot it on its first tour of duty. Imperial scouts located Strike-cruiser ''[[Nuance]]'' preparing to sell TIE Defenders to Rebel cruiser ''[[Prism]]''. Stele in his Missile Boat destroyed the T-wing fighter coverage, while the ''Nuance'' launched transports [[Miner group|''Miner'']] to deploy mines around the cruiser. The Imperial fighters overwhelmed the defenses, the ''Prism'' was destroyed, and the ''Nuance'' was boarded and captured, recovering eight TIE Defenders. Stele also discovered Rebel probe ''[[Yey]]'' and destroyed it. A few prisoners then revealed that Ali Tarrak had a [[Deep Space Manufacturing Facility|manufacturing facility]] producing TIE Defenders.

===Rebel Assault===
Thrawn, knowing that the Rebels wouldn't give up the Defenders without a fight, decided to make a stand against the Rebel counterattack. The Rebels, sure enough, sent three Carrack-cruisers ''[[Kingdom 1|Kingdom]]'' and multiple [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]], [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]], and [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. Although the ''[[Grey Wolf]]'' suffered considerable damage. Stele in his Missile Boat managed to defeat the Rebel counterattack by destroying all three Carrack-cruisers and the [[Delta-class JV-7 escort shuttle|escort shuttle]] ''[[Black Box]]'' that was carrying [[Officer/Legends|officers]].

===Eliminate TIE Defender Factory===
Wasting no time, Thrawn tracked down Tarrak's factory [[Tarrak (Deep Space Manufacturing Facility)|''Tarrak'']]. Though he intended to destroy the facility, he saw a resemblance of himself in Tarrak and convinced Palpatine that she could be useful, so he was ordered to capture her instead. After dealing with her T-wings and Y-wings Stele soon shot down some of the [[Mine Type C|Type C mines]] dodging and shooting down the missiles launched from each one. Tarrak's TIE Defenders were soon launched from the facility but were also destroyed before they could do any real damage. With the fighter defenses eliminated and soon the mines cleared [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] began their bombing run on the facility. At its end, the Empire defeated the Nami forces, destroying their TIE Defender factory, and captured their leader, Ali Tarrak, also reclaiming the TIE Defender scientist and repelling the Rebel attack from the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] and two Strike cruisers ''[[Lonsigar]]'' [[Lonsigar 1|1]] and [[Lonsigar 2|2]].

==Aftermath==
The attempt to proliferate the TIE Defenders brought under control, leaving Zaarin the only one who had TIE Defenders to fight the Empire with. The success resulted in Thrawn being promoted to [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]], placing him among Palpatine's inner circle of twelve Grand Admirals in the Imperial Navy despite the Emperor's preference for Humans over Aliens. Thrawn was then tasked to track down Zaarin and eliminate him once and for all.

Stele was awarded the [[Medal of Horror]] for his efforts.

==Behind the scenes==
New Threats is the title of Battle Ten in the single-player campaign of [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']].
